prediction_storage.notes: Calibration is a downstream transform. Never overwrite raw model outputs or raw parsed numeric scores.

one_by_one.prompt_template: Score this audio from 1-10 based on the presence of the following emotion: {emotion}
one_by_one.description: One model call per audio/emotion pair. Store raw scores for all emotions, then calibrate downstream.

one_by_one_paper_0_10.prompt_template:
Score this audio from 0-10 based on the presence of the following emotion: {emotion}

Use this scale:
0 = the emotion is absent.
5 = the emotion is mildly or ambiguously present.
10 = the emotion is strongly present.

Return only a single number from 0 to 10.
one_by_one_paper_0_10.description: One model call per audio/emotion pair using the paper-comparable 0-10 scale.

one_by_one_human_rubric.prompt_template:
Rate the presence of the following emotion in the audio: {emotion}

Use this 0-2 rubric:
0 = the emotion is not present in the audio.
1 = the emotion is weakly or ambiguously present in the audio.
2 = the emotion is clearly or strongly present in the audio.

Return only a single number: 0, 1, or 2.
one_by_one_human_rubric.description: One model call per audio/emotion pair using the same 0-2 presence scale as the human labels.

one_by_one_contrastive_rubric.prompt_template:
Rate the audio for two contrasting states:
A. {emotion}
B. {opposite_emotion}

Use this 0-2 presence rubric for each state:
0 = the state is not present in the audio.
1 = the state is weakly or ambiguously present in the audio.
2 = the state is clearly or strongly present in the audio.

Return only valid JSON in exactly this format: {{"emotion_score": 0, "opposite_score": 0}}.
one_by_one_contrastive_rubric.description: One model call per audio/emotion pair asking for both the target emotion and an explicit opposite or contrast state on the human 0-2 scale.

opposites.Longing: contented detachment or lack of desire

all_at_once.description: Paper-comparable mode: one model call per audio asking for all emotion scores.
